props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the accordion cannot be interacted with.

props.name: readonly
props.description:
If `true`, the accordion cannot be interacted with,
but does not alter the opacity.

props.name: toggleIcon
props.description:
The toggle icon to use. This icon will be
rotated when the accordion is expanded
or collapsed.

props.name: toggleIconSlot
props.description:
The slot inside of `ion-item` to
place the toggle icon. Defaults to `"end"`.

props.name: value
props.description:
The value of the accordion. Defaults to an autogenerated
value.

slots.name: content
slots.description:
Content is placed below the header and is
shown or hidden based on expanded state.

slots.name: header
slots.description:
Content is placed at the top and is used to
expand or collapse the accordion item.

props.name: animated
props.description:
If `true`, all accordions inside of the
accordion group will animate when expanding
or collapsing.

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the accordion group cannot be interacted with.

props.name: expand
props.description:
Describes the expansion behavior for each accordion.
Possible values are `"compact"` and `"inset"`.
Defaults to `"compact"`.

props.name: multiple
props.description:
If `true`, the accordion group can have multiple
accordion components expanded at the same time.

props.name: readonly
props.description:
If `true`, the accordion group cannot be interacted with,
but does not alter the opacity.

props.name: value
props.description:
The value of the accordion group. This controls which
accordions are expanded.
This should be an array of strings only when `multiple="true"`

props.name: animated
props.description: If `true`, the action sheet will animate.

props.name: backdropDismiss
props.description: If `true`, the action sheet will be dismissed when the backdrop is clicked.

props.name: buttons
props.description: An array of buttons for the action sheet.

props.name: cssClass
props.description:
Additional classes to apply for custom CSS. If multiple classes are
provided they should be separated by spaces.

props.name: enterAnimation
props.description: Animation to use when the action sheet is presented.

props.name: header
props.description: Title for the action sheet.

props.name: htmlAttributes
props.description: Additional attributes to pass to the action sheet.

props.name: isOpen
props.description:
If `true`, the action sheet will open. If `false`, the action sheet will close.
Use this if you need finer grained control over presentation, otherwise
just use the actionSheetController or the `trigger` property.
Note: `isOpen` will not automatically be set back to `false` when
the action sheet dismisses. You will need to do that in your code.

props.name: keyboardClose
props.description: If `true`, the keyboard will be automatically dismissed when the overlay is presented.

props.name: leaveAnimation
props.description: Animation to use when the action sheet is dismissed.

props.name: subHeader
props.description: Subtitle for the action sheet.

props.name: translucent
props.description:
If `true`, the action sheet will be translucent.
Only applies when the mode is `"ios"` and the device supports
[`backdrop-filter`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/backdrop-filter#Browser_compatibility).

props.name: trigger
props.description:
An ID corresponding to the trigger element that
causes the action sheet to open when clicked.

props.name: active
props.description:
If `true`, the breadcrumb will take on a different look to show that
it is the currently active breadcrumb. Defaults to `true` for the
last breadcrumb if it is not set on any.

props.name: color
props.description:
The color to use from your application's color palette.
Default options are: `"primary"`, `"secondary"`, `"tertiary"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"light"`, `"medium"`, and `"dark"`.
For more information on colors, see [theming](/docs/theming/basics).

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the user cannot interact with the breadcrumb.

props.name: download
props.description:
This attribute instructs browsers to download a URL instead of navigating to
it, so the user will be prompted to save it as a local file. If the attribute
has a value, it is used as the pre-filled file name in the Save prompt
(the user can still change the file name if they want).

props.name: href
props.description:
Contains a URL or a URL fragment that the hyperlink points to.
If this property is set, an anchor tag will be rendered.

props.name: rel
props.description:
Specifies the relationship of the target object to the link object.
The value is a space-separated list of [link types](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Link_types).

props.name: routerAnimation
props.description:
When using a router, it specifies the transition animation when navigating to
another page using `href`.

props.name: routerDirection
props.description:
When using a router, it specifies the transition direction when navigating to
another page using `href`.

props.name: separator
props.description:
If true, show a separator between this breadcrumb and the next.
Defaults to `true` for all breadcrumbs except the last.

props.name: target
props.description:
Specifies where to display the linked URL.
Only applies when an `href` is provided.
Special keywords: `"_blank"`, `"_self"`, `"_parent"`, `"_top"`.

props.name: color
props.description:
The color to use from your application's color palette.
Default options are: `"primary"`, `"secondary"`, `"tertiary"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"light"`, `"medium"`, and `"dark"`.
For more information on colors, see [theming](/docs/theming/basics).

props.name: fixedSlotPlacement
props.description:
Controls where the fixed content is placed relative to the main content
in the DOM. This can be used to control the order in which fixed elements
receive keyboard focus.
For example, if a FAB in the fixed slot should receive keyboard focus before
the main page content, set this property to `'before'`.

props.name: forceOverscroll
props.description:
If `true` and the content does not cause an overflow scroll, the scroll interaction will cause a bounce.
If the content exceeds the bounds of ionContent, nothing will change.
Note, this does not disable the system bounce on iOS. That is an OS level setting.

props.name: fullscreen
props.description:
If `true`, the content will scroll behind the headers
and footers. This effect can easily be seen by setting the toolbar
to transparent.

props.name: scrollEvents
props.description:
Because of performance reasons, ionScroll events are disabled by default, in order to enable them
and start listening from (ionScroll), set this property to `true`.

props.name: scrollX
props.description: If you want to enable the content scrolling in the X axis, set this property to `true`.

props.name: scrollY
props.description: If you want to disable the content scrolling in the Y axis, set this property to `false`.

slots.name: default
slots.description: - Content is placed in the scrollable area if provided without a slot.

slots.name: fixed
slots.description: Should be used for fixed content that should not scroll.

props.name: autocapitalize
props.description:
Indicates whether and how the text value should be automatically capitalized as it is entered/edited by the user.
Available options: `"off"`, `"none"`, `"on"`, `"sentences"`, `"words"`, `"characters"`.

props.name: autocomplete
props.description: Indicates whether the value of the control can be automatically completed by the browser.

props.name: autocorrect
props.description: Whether auto correction should be enabled when the user is entering/editing the text value.

props.name: autofocus
props.description:
Sets the [`autofocus` attribute](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/autofocus) on the native input element.

This may not be sufficient for the element to be focused on page load. See [managing focus](/docs/developing/managing-focus) for more information.

props.name: clearInput
props.description: If `true`, a clear icon will appear in the input when there is a value. Clicking it clears the input.

props.name: clearInputIcon
props.description: The icon to use for the clear button. Only applies when `clearInput` is set to `true`.

props.name: clearOnEdit
props.description: If `true`, the value will be cleared after focus upon edit. Defaults to `true` when `type` is `"password"`, `false` for all other types.

props.name: color
props.description:
The color to use from your application's color palette.
Default options are: `"primary"`, `"secondary"`, `"tertiary"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"light"`, `"medium"`, and `"dark"`.
For more information on colors, see [theming](/docs/theming/basics).

props.name: counter
props.description: If `true`, a character counter will display the ratio of characters used and the total character limit. Developers must also set the `maxlength` property for the counter to be calculated correctly.

props.name: counterFormatter
props.description:
A callback used to format the counter text.
By default the counter text is set to "itemLength / maxLength".

See https://ionicframework.com/docs/troubleshooting/runtime#accessing-this
if you need to access `this` from within the callback.

props.name: debounce
props.description: Set the amount of time, in milliseconds, to wait to trigger the `ionInput` event after each keystroke.

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the user cannot interact with the input.

props.name: enterkeyhint
props.description:
A hint to the browser for which enter key to display.
Possible values: `"enter"`, `"done"`, `"go"`, `"next"`,
`"previous"`, `"search"`, and `"send"`.

props.name: errorText
props.description: Text that is placed under the input and displayed when an error is detected.

props.name: fill
props.description:
The fill for the item. If `"solid"` the item will have a background. If
`"outline"` the item will be transparent with a border. Only available in `md` mode.

props.name: helperText
props.description: Text that is placed under the input and displayed when no error is detected.

props.name: inputmode
props.description:
A hint to the browser for which keyboard to display.
Possible values: `"none"`, `"text"`, `"tel"`, `"url"`,
`"email"`, `"numeric"`, `"decimal"`, and `"search"`.

props.name: label
props.description:
The visible label associated with the input.

Use this if you need to render a plaintext label.

The `label` property will take priority over the `label` slot if both are used.

props.name: labelPlacement
props.description:
Where to place the label relative to the input.
`"start"`: The label will appear to the left of the input in LTR and to the right in RTL.
`"end"`: The label will appear to the right of the input in LTR and to the left in RTL.
`"floating"`: The label will appear smaller and above the input when the input is focused or it has a value. Otherwise it will appear on top of the input.
`"stacked"`: The label will appear smaller and above the input regardless even when the input is blurred or has no value.
`"fixed"`: The label has the same behavior as `"start"` except it also has a fixed width. Long text will be truncated with ellipses ("...").

props.name: max
props.description: The maximum value, which must not be less than its minimum (min attribute) value.

props.name: maxlength
props.description: If the value of the type attribute is `text`, `email`, `search`, `password`, `tel`, or `url`, this attribute specifies the maximum number of characters that the user can enter.

props.name: min
props.description: The minimum value, which must not be greater than its maximum (max attribute) value.

props.name: minlength
props.description: If the value of the type attribute is `text`, `email`, `search`, `password`, `tel`, or `url`, this attribute specifies the minimum number of characters that the user can enter.

props.name: multiple
props.description: If `true`, the user can enter more than one value. This attribute applies when the type attribute is set to `"email"`, otherwise it is ignored.

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the control, which is submitted with the form data.

props.name: pattern
props.description: A regular expression that the value is checked against. The pattern must match the entire value, not just some subset. Use the title attribute to describe the pattern to help the user. This attribute applies when the value of the type attribute is `"text"`, `"search"`, `"tel"`, `"url"`, `"email"`, `"date"`, or `"password"`, otherwise it is ignored. When the type attribute is `"date"`, `pattern` will only be used in browsers that do not support the `"date"` input type natively. See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/date for more information.

props.name: placeholder
props.description:
Instructional text that shows before the input has a value.
This property applies only when the `type` property is set to `"email"`,
`"number"`, `"password"`, `"search"`, `"tel"`, `"text"`, or `"url"`, otherwise it is ignored.

props.name: readonly
props.description: If `true`, the user cannot modify the value.

props.name: required
props.description: If `true`, the user must fill in a value before submitting a form.

props.name: shape
props.description: The shape of the input. If "round" it will have an increased border radius.

props.name: spellcheck
props.description: If `true`, the element will have its spelling and grammar checked.

props.name: step
props.description:
Works with the min and max attributes to limit the increments at which a value can be set.
Possible values are: `"any"` or a positive floating point number.

props.name: type
props.description: The type of control to display. The default type is text.

props.name: value
props.description: The value of the input.

slots.name: end
slots.description: Content to display at the trailing edge of the input. (EXPERIMENTAL)

slots.name: label
slots.description: The label text to associate with the input. Use the `labelPlacement` property to control where the label is placed relative to the input. Use this if you need to render a label with custom HTML. (EXPERIMENTAL)

slots.name: start
slots.description: Content to display at the leading edge of the input. (EXPERIMENTAL)

props.name: color
props.description:
The color to use from your application's color palette.
Default options are: `"primary"`, `"secondary"`, `"tertiary"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"light"`, `"medium"`, and `"dark"`.
For more information on colors, see [theming](/docs/theming/basics).

props.name: lines
props.description: How the bottom border should be displayed on the list header.

props.name: animated
props.description: If `true`, the picker will animate.

props.name: backdropDismiss
props.description: If `true`, the picker will be dismissed when the backdrop is clicked.

props.name: buttons
props.description: Array of buttons to be displayed at the top of the picker.

props.name: columns
props.description: Array of columns to be displayed in the picker.

props.name: cssClass
props.description:
Additional classes to apply for custom CSS. If multiple classes are
provided they should be separated by spaces.

props.name: duration
props.description: Number of milliseconds to wait before dismissing the picker.

props.name: enterAnimation
props.description: Animation to use when the picker is presented.

props.name: htmlAttributes
props.description: Additional attributes to pass to the picker.

props.name: isOpen
props.description:
If `true`, the picker will open. If `false`, the picker will close.
Use this if you need finer grained control over presentation, otherwise
just use the pickerController or the `trigger` property.
Note: `isOpen` will not automatically be set back to `false` when
the picker dismisses. You will need to do that in your code.

props.name: keyboardClose
props.description: If `true`, the keyboard will be automatically dismissed when the overlay is presented.

props.name: leaveAnimation
props.description: Animation to use when the picker is dismissed.

props.name: showBackdrop
props.description: If `true`, a backdrop will be displayed behind the picker.

props.name: trigger
props.description:
An ID corresponding to the trigger element that
causes the picker to open when clicked.

props.name: type
props.description:
Sets the type of ripple-effect:

- `bounded`: the ripple effect expands from the user's click position
- `unbounded`: the ripple effect expands from the center of the button and overflows the container.

NOTE: Surfaces for bounded ripples should have the overflow property set to hidden,
while surfaces for unbounded ripples should have it set to visible.

props.name: cancelText
props.description: The text to display on the cancel button.

props.name: color
props.description:
The color to use from your application's color palette.
Default options are: `"primary"`, `"secondary"`, `"tertiary"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"light"`, `"medium"`, and `"dark"`.
For more information on colors, see [theming](/docs/theming/basics).

This property is only available when using the modern select syntax.

props.name: compareWith
props.description:
This property allows developers to specify a custom function or property
name for comparing objects when determining the selected option in the
ion-select. When not specified, the default behavior will use strict
equality (===) for comparison.

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the user cannot interact with the select.

props.name: errorText
props.description: Text that is placed under the select and displayed when an error is detected.

props.name: expandedIcon
props.description:
The toggle icon to show when the select is open. If defined, the icon
rotation behavior in `md` mode will be disabled. If undefined, `toggleIcon`
will be used for when the select is both open and closed.

props.name: fill
props.description:
The fill for the item. If `"solid"` the item will have a background. If
`"outline"` the item will be transparent with a border. Only available in `md` mode.

props.name: helperText
props.description: Text that is placed under the select and displayed when no error is detected.

props.name: interface
props.description: The interface the select should use: `action-sheet`, `popover`, `alert`, or `modal`.

props.name: interfaceOptions
props.description:
Any additional options that the `alert`, `action-sheet` or `popover` interface
can take. See the [ion-alert docs](./alert), the
[ion-action-sheet docs](./action-sheet), the
[ion-popover docs](./popover), and the [ion-modal docs](./modal) for the
create options for each interface.

Note: `interfaceOptions` will not override `inputs` or `buttons` with the `alert` interface.

props.name: justify
props.description:
How to pack the label and select within a line.
`justify` does not apply when the label and select
are on different lines when `labelPlacement` is set to
`"floating"` or `"stacked"`.
`"start"`: The label and select will appear on the left in LTR and
on the right in RTL.
`"end"`: The label and select will appear on the right in LTR and
on the left in RTL.
`"space-between"`: The label and select will appear on opposite
ends of the line with space between the two elements.

props.name: label
props.description:
The visible label associated with the select.

Use this if you need to render a plaintext label.

The `label` property will take priority over the `label` slot if both are used.

props.name: labelPlacement
props.description:
Where to place the label relative to the select.
`"start"`: The label will appear to the left of the select in LTR and to the right in RTL.
`"end"`: The label will appear to the right of the select in LTR and to the left in RTL.
`"floating"`: The label will appear smaller and above the select when the select is focused or it has a value. Otherwise it will appear on top of the select.
`"stacked"`: The label will appear smaller and above the select regardless even when the select is blurred or has no value.
`"fixed"`: The label has the same behavior as `"start"` except it also has a fixed width. Long text will be truncated with ellipses ("...").
When using `"floating"` or `"stacked"` we recommend initializing the select with either a `value` or a `placeholder`.

props.name: multiple
props.description: If `true`, the select can accept multiple values.

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the control, which is submitted with the form data.

props.name: okText
props.description: The text to display on the ok button.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: The text to display when the select is empty.

props.name: required
props.description:
If true, screen readers will announce it as a required field. This property
works only for accessibility purposes, it will not prevent the form from
submitting if the value is invalid.

props.name: selectedText
props.description: The text to display instead of the selected option's value.

props.name: shape
props.description: The shape of the select. If "round" it will have an increased border radius.

props.name: toggleIcon
props.description:
The toggle icon to use. Defaults to `chevronExpand` for `ios` mode,
or `caretDownSharp` for `md` mode.

props.name: value
props.description: The value of the select.

slots.name: end
slots.description: Content to display at the trailing edge of the select.

slots.name: label
slots.description: The label text to associate with the select. Use the `labelPlacement` property to control where the label is placed relative to the select. Use this if you need to render a label with custom HTML.

slots.name: start
slots.description: Content to display at the leading edge of the select.

props.name: autocapitalize
props.description:
Indicates whether and how the text value should be automatically capitalized as it is entered/edited by the user.
Available options: `"off"`, `"none"`, `"on"`, `"sentences"`, `"words"`, `"characters"`.

props.name: autofocus
props.description:
Sets the [`autofocus` attribute](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Global_attributes/autofocus) on the native input element.

This may not be sufficient for the element to be focused on page load. See [managing focus](/docs/developing/managing-focus) for more information.

props.name: autoGrow
props.description:
If `true`, the textarea container will grow and shrink based
on the contents of the textarea.

props.name: clearOnEdit
props.description: If `true`, the value will be cleared after focus upon edit.

props.name: color
props.description:
The color to use from your application's color palette.
Default options are: `"primary"`, `"secondary"`, `"tertiary"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"light"`, `"medium"`, and `"dark"`.
For more information on colors, see [theming](/docs/theming/basics).

props.name: cols
props.description: The visible width of the text control, in average character widths. If it is specified, it must be a positive integer.

props.name: counter
props.description:
If `true`, a character counter will display the ratio of characters used and the total character limit.
Developers must also set the `maxlength` property for the counter to be calculated correctly.

props.name: counterFormatter
props.description:
A callback used to format the counter text.
By default the counter text is set to "itemLength / maxLength".

See https://ionicframework.com/docs/troubleshooting/runtime#accessing-this
if you need to access `this` from within the callback.

props.name: debounce
props.description: Set the amount of time, in milliseconds, to wait to trigger the `ionInput` event after each keystroke.

props.name: disabled
props.description: If `true`, the user cannot interact with the textarea.

props.name: enterkeyhint
props.description:
A hint to the browser for which enter key to display.
Possible values: `"enter"`, `"done"`, `"go"`, `"next"`,
`"previous"`, `"search"`, and `"send"`.

props.name: errorText
props.description: Text that is placed under the textarea and displayed when an error is detected.

props.name: fill
props.description:
The fill for the item. If `"solid"` the item will have a background. If
`"outline"` the item will be transparent with a border. Only available in `md` mode.

props.name: helperText
props.description: Text that is placed under the textarea and displayed when no error is detected.

props.name: inputmode
props.description:
A hint to the browser for which keyboard to display.
Possible values: `"none"`, `"text"`, `"tel"`, `"url"`,
`"email"`, `"numeric"`, `"decimal"`, and `"search"`.

props.name: label
props.description:
The visible label associated with the textarea.

Use this if you need to render a plaintext label.

The `label` property will take priority over the `label` slot if both are used.

props.name: labelPlacement
props.description:
Where to place the label relative to the textarea.
`"start"`: The label will appear to the left of the textarea in LTR and to the right in RTL.
`"end"`: The label will appear to the right of the textarea in LTR and to the left in RTL.
`"floating"`: The label will appear smaller and above the textarea when the textarea is focused or it has a value. Otherwise it will appear on top of the textarea.
`"stacked"`: The label will appear smaller and above the textarea regardless even when the textarea is blurred or has no value.
`"fixed"`: The label has the same behavior as `"start"` except it also has a fixed width. Long text will be truncated with ellipses ("...").

props.name: maxlength
props.description: This attribute specifies the maximum number of characters that the user can enter.

props.name: minlength
props.description: This attribute specifies the minimum number of characters that the user can enter.

props.name: name
props.description: The name of the control, which is submitted with the form data.

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Instructional text that shows before the input has a value.

props.name: readonly
props.description: If `true`, the user cannot modify the value.

props.name: required
props.description: If `true`, the user must fill in a value before submitting a form.

props.name: rows
props.description: The number of visible text lines for the control.

props.name: shape
props.description: The shape of the textarea. If "round" it will have an increased border radius.

props.name: spellcheck
props.description: If `true`, the element will have its spelling and grammar checked.

props.name: value
props.description: The value of the textarea.

props.name: wrap
props.description: Indicates how the control wraps text.

slots.name: end
slots.description: Content to display at the trailing edge of the textarea. (EXPERIMENTAL)

slots.name: label
slots.description: The label text to associate with the textarea. Use the `labelPlacement` property to control where the label is placed relative to the textarea. Use this if you need to render a label with custom HTML. (EXPERIMENTAL)

slots.name: start
slots.description: Content to display at the leading edge of the textarea. (EXPERIMENTAL)

expectedNotes.propNotes: Sixteen declared overlay props cover internal overlay state, presentation controls, buttons, headers, animations, and `trigger`.
Thirteen declared props mix internal breadcrumb state (`collapsed`, `last`, `showCollapsedIndicator`) with anchor, router, and appearance controls.
expectedNotes.slotNotes: Default slot plus named `start`, `end`, and `separator` slots.

expectedNotes.propNotes: Seven public props cover color, fullscreen and overscroll behavior, fixed slot placement, and scroll axis and event toggles.
expectedNotes.slotNotes: Default scrollable content slot plus named `fixed` slot.

expectedNotes.propNotes: A broad native-input-derived prop surface covers labels, helper and error text, counters, native input attributes, styling, and validation flags.
Sixteen declared overlay props cover internal overlay state, presentation controls, buttons and columns, and trigger-driven opening.
Single `type` prop with `bounded` and `unbounded` options, defaulting to `bounded`.
A large select-control surface covers interface selection, labels, helper and error text, comparison hooks, value handling, and accessibility styling flags.
A large textarea surface covers labels, helper and error text, counters, autogrow behavior, native textarea attributes, and validation flags.
